logic: Land must not be forest at project start. Must meet host-country forest definition if applicable.

conditions: Land documented as non-forest at project start date
If host-country forest definition applies, land must not meet it for 10+ years prior to project start
Mangrove areas on tidal wetlands excluded from ARR under this methodology
exceptions: Land is in a wetland or peatland that qualifies as forest under VCS Program definitions

source_span_text: Project area must not be forest at project start. If the host country applies a forest definition, the land must not meet that definition for at least 10 years prior to the project start date. Mangrove areas on tidal wetlands are excluded from ARR eligibility.

logic: Land must be non-forest or managed non-forest for 10+ years. Excludes wetlands, peatlands, and certain AR-ACM0003 exclusion categories.

conditions: Land must be non-forest at project start for 10+ years
Tidal wetland areas excluded (condition 4)
Organic soil/water table manipulation excluded unless combined with WRC methodology (condition 5)
Mechanical removal or burning of pre-existing dead wood prohibited unless all material remains onsite (condition 3)
exceptions: Wetland activities may be combined with VM0036 or WRC methodology under condition 5

source_span_text: This methodology is not applicable under the following conditions: 3) Project activities involve mechanical removal offsite or burning of significant stocks of pre-existing dead wood. 4) Project activities take place in tidal wetlands (e.g., mangroves, salt marshes). 5) Project activities that occur on organic soils or in wetlands and result in a manipulation of the water table.

logic: Two approaches: area-based (plot sampling) or census-based (complete enumeration). Must select based on project characteristics.

conditions: Area-based approach selected when plot-based sampling is feasible and project area is the known multiplier
Census-based approach selected when the project area can be exhaustively enumerated as discrete planting units
exceptions: Project does not meet the applicability conditions of either approach

source_span_text: The methodology provides two quantification approaches: area-based approach using traditional plot-based sampling methods that scale biomass estimates per unit area to the project level using project area as the multiplier; and census-based approach using a complete census of planting units where the project area is exhaustively enumerated.

logic: Project boundary must be fixed ex-ante. Stratification by land cover, soil type, and management regime.

conditions: Project boundary must be fixed ex-ante and documented in PD
Stratification by land cover, soil type, and management regime is mandatory
Project area cannot change over the project crediting period
exceptions: Boundary revision required due to force majeure or documented error

source_span_text: The project boundary shall be determined and documented in the project description. The project area must be fixed ex-ante and cannot change over the project lifetime. Stratification by land cover, soil type, and management regime is required.

logic: AGB and BGB mandatory. Litter, dead wood, SOC optional per Appendix 2 significance test (built-in, replaces external T-SIG). If included in baseline must also be in project scenario.

conditions: AGB and BGB are mandatory carbon pools
Litter, dead wood, and SOC may be excluded if Appendix 2 significance test shows their contribution is <5% of total decreases and <5% of removals
If a carbon pool is included in the baseline scenario, it must also be included in the project scenario
exceptions: Pools determined significant by Appendix 2 procedure must be included regardless of project preference

source_span_text: Individual carbon pools and emissions sources may be neglected if the relative contribution of decreases in a carbon pool or increases in an emissions source is less than 5 percent of the sum total of all decreases in carbon pools and increases in emissions, or less than 5 percent of carbon dioxide removals, whichever is smaller. The procedure in Appendix 2 (Equation A7) provides the stepwise significance test replacing the external T-SIG tool.

logic: Two methods: 1) Performance Method via dynamic benchmark, 2) Project Method via VT0001 additionality + baseline scenario analysis.

conditions: Area-based approach uses performance benchmark from Appendix 1
Census-based approach: pre-existing woody biomass <10% or continuous cropping area
Census-based crediting baseline may be set to zero if criteria are met
exceptions: Area-based approach may not apply where control plots cannot be established as representative

source_span_text: Area-based approach: A performance benchmark is used to set the crediting baseline, defined as the business-as-usual increase in vegetative cover relative to the project, based on data from representative control plots. Census-based approach: The project activity must occur within an area with pre-existing woody biomass cover of less than ten percent and/or in an area subject to continuous cropping. If these criteria are met, the crediting baseline may be set to zero.

logic: Project Method requires VT0001 demonstration. Performance Method uses automatic additionality via benchmark exceedance.

conditions: Area-based: regulatory surplus + performance benchmark (Z test, Appendix 1)
Area-based: investment barrier only when non-carbon revenues exist
Census-based: regulatory surplus + investment barrier + common practice (<15% adoption rate)
Additionality must be reassessed at each verification for area-based approach
exceptions: Performance method demonstrates additionality automatically via benchmark exceedance without VT0001

source_span_text: The projects must apply a performance method (area-based approach) or a project method (census-based approach) for the demonstration of additionality. Area-based approach: Step 1 Regulatory surplus, Step 2 Performance benchmark, Step 3 Investment barrier (only when revenues/incentives exist). Census-based approach: Step 1 Regulatory surplus, Step 3 Investment barrier, Step 4 Common practice.

logic: Removals = min(project C change, project C change x (1 - PB)) - leakage - uncertainty. PB from ex-post reference region observations.

conditions: Removals = min(project C change, project C change x (1 - PB)) - leakage - uncertainty. PB from ex-post reference region observations.
exceptions: Performance benchmark PB_t may be negative; minimum function ensures CRt does not exceed DeltaC_WP_t

source_span_text: Carbon dioxide removals are calculated by comparing the carbon stock at the current monitoring year (t) to the carbon stock at t minus the length of the monitoring interval. Area-based approach: CRt = MIN(DeltaC_WP_t, DeltaC_WP_t x (1 - PB_t)) - LK_t - UNC_t. Census-based approach: CRt = DeltaC_WP_t - PE_t - UNC_t. Removals are annualized by dividing CRt by the monitoring interval length.

logic: Activity shifting leakage must be assessed. Market leakage is de minimis for ARR. Fossil fuel and biomass burning emissions must be included.

conditions: Activity shifting leakage must be assessed and deducted
Market leakage is de minimis for ARR and may be neglected
Fossil fuel combustion and biomass burning emissions must be included in project emissions
exceptions: No leakage deduction required if leakage prevention activities are demonstrated effective

source_span_text: Leakage shall be assessed and deducted from net carbon stock changes. Activity shifting leakage must be assessed using the leakage module. Market leakage is considered de minimis for ARR project activities and may be neglected. Emissions from fossil fuel combustion and biomass burning must be included as project emissions.

logic: 15% threshold at 90% CI. If exceeded, excess deducted proportionally from removals. Below threshold, no deduction.

conditions: Uncertainty threshold: 15% at 90% confidence interval
If uncertainty exceeds 15%, excess deducted proportionally from removals
If uncertainty is 15% or below, no deduction applied
exceptions: Stratification may be used to reduce uncertainty below the threshold

source_span_text: Uncertainty analysis must be carried out to ensure conservative estimates. A 15% uncertainty threshold is applied at the 90% confidence interval. If the uncertainty exceeds 15%, the excess is deducted proportionally from the net carbon removals. If uncertainty is at or below 15%, no deduction is required.

logic: Four mandatory components: data list, QA/QC, responsibilities, archiving. REDD MRV procedures via M-REDD module.

conditions: Monitoring plan must include data collection procedures
Monitoring plan must include QA/QC procedures
Monitoring plan must include data archiving and record keeping procedures
Monitoring plan must identify roles and responsibilities
exceptions: Simplified monitoring may apply where project area changes are below materiality threshold

source_span_text: The monitoring plan shall include data collection procedures, quality assurance and quality control (QA/QC) procedures, data archiving and record keeping procedures, and identification of roles and responsibilities. The monitoring plan must follow the requirements in Section 9.3 of the methodology.
